name: Node-RED
what: Flow-based programming and automation platform with strong IoT, MQTT, and message-routing heritage.
solves: Event routing, protocol bridging, device automation, MQTT-driven workflows, and low-code integrations.
users: IoT, OT, and integration teams that need visual event/message flows.
fit: Useful later if Hylono builds device telemetry or MQTT-heavy workflows. For current business/process automation, n8n is the better default.
runtime: Node.js runtime hosting flows, connectors, dashboards (if used), and integration nodes.
components: Editor/runtime, flows, nodes/plugins, optional dashboard/UI, and persistence for flow definitions.
deploy: Docker or simple service deployment is straightforward. Governance matters more than install.
deps: Persistent storage for flows, broker/API endpoints, optional MQTT, and plugin/node governance.
datastores: Local flow storage or attached storage; optional external systems for state.
auth: Admin auth, node credentials, and network/proxy protection.
apis: HTTP endpoints, MQTT, industrial/IoT protocols, and extensive node ecosystem.
security: Visual automation plus third-party nodes can create hidden attack surface. Restrict editor access and review nodes carefully.
setup_path: Use official install docs only if an IoT/protocol need justifies it. Otherwise prefer n8n.
env: Editor/base URL, auth, storage path, node credentials, broker settings, and reverse-proxy config.
persistence: Back up flows and credentials storage; version control exports for critical flows.
operations: Node/plugin review, flow testing, and environment separation matter.
failure_points: Creating two competing automation platforms without a clear reason, or relying on community nodes without security review.
integration: Best fit with Mosquitto, ThingsBoard, device telemetry, and protocol bridges. Limited need for pure business automation if n8n is already core.
verify: Confirm whether device/IoT workflows are near-term enough to justify Node-RED beside n8n.
risk: Moderate overlap risk, low infrastructure risk.
gdpr: Mostly depends on payloads flowing through it.
overlap: n8n should remain the default for business workflows; Node-RED is for IoT/protocol-heavy use cases.
codex_read_first: Read n8n and the IoT stack packages together before deciding on Node-RED.
codex_best_path: Only introduce Node-RED when MQTT/device protocol bridging becomes a real requirement.
codex_never_assume: Never assume another automation surface is free of governance cost.
